item.title: BCI startup Neurable looks to license its ‘mind-reading’ tech for consumer wearables
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: The startup specializes in "non-invasive" "mind-reading" tech—a kind of neural data collection that, its CEO hopes, will have all sorts of consumer applications.

item.title: Red Hat’s OpenClaw maintainer just made enterprise Claw deployments a lot safer
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Tank OS puts OpenClaw AI agents into a container that let's it run reliably and more safely, especially for those running fleets of them.

item.title: Otter’s new feature lets users search across their enterprise tools
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: Otter is also releasing a new Windows app that can capture meeting notes without joining one

item.title: OpenAI ends Microsoft legal peril over its $50B Amazon deal
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: OpenAI has won major concessions from its largest shareholder, Microsoft, that will allow it to sell products on AWS, while Microsoft gets more cash in a revenue-share agreement.

item.title: DeepMind’s David Silver just raised $1.1B to build an AI that learns without human data
item.dc:creator: Anna Heim
item.description: Ineffable Intelligence, a British AI lab founded a mere few months ago by former DeepMind researcher David Silver, has raised $1.1 billion in funding at a valuation of $5.1 billion.

item.title: Investors back Skye’s AI home screen app for iPhone ahead of launch
item.dc:creator: Sarah Perez
item.description: Skye's new AI app attracted investors before it even launched — a sign of interest in a more AI-aware iPhone.

item.title: China blocks Meta’s $2B Manus deal after months-long probe
item.dc:creator: Kate Park
item.description: China has ordered Meta to unwind its multibillion-dollar Manus acquisition, dealing a potential setback to Zuckerberg’s push into AI agents.

item.title: OpenAI could be making a phone with AI agents replacing apps
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: There have been plenty of rumors about OpenAI's hardware plans, which involve launching a pair of earbuds. A new note from industry analyst Ming-Chi Kuo suggests that the AI company might be working on a phone in collaboration with MediaTek, Qualcomm, and Luxshare.

item.title: Meta inks deal for solar power at night, beamed from space
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: Overview Energy's first contract with Meta is a small step toward a future of space-based solar power.

item.title: To buy this Bay Area home, you’ll need Anthropic equity
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: Someone’s offering an unusual deal for a 13-acre property in Mill Valley, just north of San Francisco.

item.title: Anthropic created a test marketplace for agent-on-agent commerce
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: In a recent experiment, Anthropic created a classified marketplace where AI agents represented both buyers and sellers, striking real deals for real goods and real money.

item.title: Maine’s governor vetoes data center moratorium
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: L.D. 307 would have imposed the country’s first statewide moratorium on new data centers — lasting, in this case, until November 1, 2027.

item.title: OpenAI CEO apologizes to Tumbler Ridge community
item.dc:creator: Anthony Ha
item.description: In a letter to the residents of Tumbler Ridge, Canada, OpenAI CEO Sam Altman said he is “deeply sorry” that his company failed to alert law enforcement about the suspect in a recent mass shooting.

item.title: Why Cohere is merging with Aleph Alpha
item.dc:creator: Anna Heim
item.description: Canadian AI startup Cohere is taking over Germany-based Aleph Alpha with support from Schwarz Group. With the blessing of their governments, the companies intend to offer a sovereign alternative to enterprises in an AI landscape dominated by American players.

item.title: Why Tokyo is the most important tech destination of 2026
item.dc:creator: Cindy Zackney
item.description: SusHi Tech Tokyo 2026 has four tightly defined technology domains, each backed by live demonstrations, dedicated exhibit floors, and sessions featuring the people actually building and funding these technologies globally.

item.title: Apple under Ternus: what comes next for the tech giant’s hardware strategy
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: John Ternus, Apple's incoming CEO, is a hardware guy, signaling Apple may be putting devices back at the center of its strategy.

item.title: Meta’s loss is Thinking Machines’ gain
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: Meta has been poaching talent from Thinking Machines Lab. But it's a two-way street.

item.title: ComfyUI hits $500M valuation as creators seek more control over AI-generated media
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: ComfyUI, whose tools give creators more control over AI image, video, and audio generation, just raised $30 million.

item.title: Google to invest up to $40B in Anthropic in cash and compute
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
item.description: Google plans up to $40B investment in Anthropic as AI rivals race to secure massive compute capacity, following the limited release of its powerful, cybersecurity-focused Mythos model.

item.title: Apple’s new CEO, and why Elon Musk wants to buy Cursor for $60B
item.dc:creator: Theresa Loconsolo, Kirsten Korosec, Anthony Ha, Sean O'Kane
item.description: A new era is on the way for Apple as Tim Cook plans to step down from his CEO role in September, handing the reins to hardware chief John Ternus.   Ternus may be inheriting one of the most durable businesses in tech, but he’s also stepping into a very different ecosystem than the one Cook spent decades shaping. The App […]
